note: Full military casualty breakdown was not officially released; records were sealed for five years. SSPC reported 8 total killed across all categories combined; military-specific count estimated from partial disclosures.
Government reported cartel casualties but withheld individual breakdowns. Local media accounts suggest higher cartel deaths; many bodies were reportedly removed by the cartel before official tallies.

source: SSPC (1 official) / Local authorities (3 in Tres Ríos alone)
note: Government acknowledged only 1 civilian death; local officials cited at least 3 in the Tres Ríos neighborhood. Civilian bystanders were caught in crossfire and carjackings across the city. True civilian toll remains unknown.

Official government figure of 8 dead and 16 wounded. Local officials and witness accounts suggest 13–15+ dead. Sealed operational records prevent definitive accounting. 68 military vehicles were damaged.

Includes Colonel Juan José Moreno Orzua and his four bodyguards (ambushed in Escuinapa) plus five additional soldiers killed in Culiacán firefights. This was the most severe military toll in any single cartel operation in years.
SEDENA reported 19 confirmed cartel gunmen killed. Independent monitors suggested the figure may be understated as the cartel customarily removes casualties from scenes before official accounting.
One police officer killed during statewide cartel operations across Sinaloa in response to Ovidio's arrest. Reported separately from military casualties.

Official SEDENA total: 10 soldiers + 19 cartel gunmen + 1 police = 30 dead. 35+ wounded; 21 arrested. Weapons seized included four .50 caliber Barrett rifles and six .50 caliber machine guns. Some independent counts cited 29.

Approximately 1,972 homicides in Sinaloa over 12 months of the Chapitos vs. La Mayiza civil war — averaging 5.4 deaths per day, a 200%+ increase over the prior year. Additionally ~1,949 kidnapping victims. Official Sinaloa state figures are lower. This conflict was triggered by the arrests of Los Chapitos and El Mayo.

51 inmates escaped from Aguaruto Federal Penitentiary during the October 2019 Culiacanazo; 2 were quickly recaptured, leaving 49 at large. Five guards had their weapons stripped during the coordinated prison riot.
